examples.text: لَا حَوْل وَلَا قُوَّة إِلَّا بِٱللّٰه.
examples.english: There is no strength or power except in God.

senses.gloss: to change, to transform, to convert, to turn (into), to make

examples.english: It will turn the earth into paradise.

senses.gloss: to remit, to send, to transmit
to pass on, to hand on, to forward
to divert, to distract, to deflect
to turn off, to switch off, to disconnect
to turn the helm, to change course

examples.text: فَبِمَا رَحۡمَةࣲ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ لِنتَ لَهُمۡ ۖ وَلَوۡ كُنتَ فَظًّا غَلِيظَ ٱلۡقَلۡبِ لَٱنفَضُّوا۟ مِنۡ حَوۡلِكَ ۖ فَٱعۡفُ عَنۡهُمۡ وَٱسۡتَغۡفِرۡ لَهُمۡ وَشَاوِرۡهُمۡ فِي ٱلۡأَمۡرِ ۖ فَإِذَا عَزَمۡتَ فَتَوَكَّلۡ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلۡمُتَوَكِّلِينَ

senses.gloss: to have strabismus, to be cross-eyed, to squint eyes

etymology.text: Compare Ugaritic 𐎈𐎍 (ḥl), Hebrew חַיִל (kháyil, “strength, might”).
